1→# Review: deepmind/87.lean
     2→
     3→## 1. Copyright Header
     4→
     5→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
     6→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
     7→
     8→## 2. Import Statement
     9→
    10→- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
    11→- [x] No other imports are present.
    12→
    13→## 3. Module Docstring
    14→
    15→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    16→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 87`.
    17→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/87`.
    18→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (weak and strong forms, plus historical note about k=4 failure).
    19→- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Er95]` with full citation.
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos87 ... end Erdos87`.
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos87`).
    25→- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block.
    26→
    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- [x] `ContainsSubgraph` has a LaTeX docstring.
    30→- [x] `graphRamseyNumber` has a LaTeX docstring.
    31→- [x] `diagRamsey` has a LaTeX docstring.
    32→- [x] `graphRamseyNumber` and `diagRamsey` are marked `noncomputable`. (`ContainsSubgraph` is a `Prop`, so it does not need `noncomputable`.)
    33→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    34→- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `ContainsSubgraph`.
    35→- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase`: `graphRamseyNumber`, `diagRamsey`.
    36→- [x] No local notation used (none needed here).
    37→
    38→## 6. Theorem Statements
    39→
    40→- [x] Both statements use the `theorem` keyword.
    41→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_87` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    42→- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
    43→- [x] Both theorems have docstrings referencing the source paper (`[Er95, p. 14]`).
    44→
    45→### `answer()` usage
    46→
    47→The original problem asks "Is it true that …?" — this is a yes/no question, so **Case 1** applies.
    48→
    49→- [x] Main theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (Case 1 pattern).
    50→- [x] Variant theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (Case 1 pattern).
    51→- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked, so `answer(True)` would mean "yes".
    52→- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ ε`, `∃ K`, `∀ k`, `∀ {V}`, etc.) appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
    53→
    54→## 7. Category Attribute
    55→
    56→- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
    57→- [x] Category value is `research open` — valid.
    58→- [x] Both `research open` theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies.
    59→
    60→## 8. AMS Attribute
    61→
    62→- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
    63→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (`5`).
    64→- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number — trivially satisfied).
    65→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    66→- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics).
    67→
    68→## 9. Proof Bodies
    69→
    70→- [x] Both open conjectures use `by` / `sorry` (multi-line form with indented `sorry` — acceptable).
    71→- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    72→- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems.
    73→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    74→
    75→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    76→
    77→- [x] Variant named `erdos_87.variants.strong` (correct naming convention).
    78→- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
    79→- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research open, AMS 5]` attributes.
    80→- [x] Variant has a docstring describing the stronger form of the conjecture.
    81→
    82→## 11. Code Quality
    83→
    84→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    85→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `∧`, `↔`, `→`, `ℝ`, `ℕ`).
    86→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    87→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    88→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (<100 characters).
    89→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    90→
    91→## 12. Build Verification
    92→
    93→- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review (requires build environment).
    94→- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→**Result: PASS** — All checkable items pass. The file is clean, well-structured, and fully conformant. The only unchecked boxes are build verification items that require running `lake build`.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/87.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 87`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/87`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (weak and strong forms, plus historical note about k=4 failure).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Er95]` with full citation.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos87 ... end Erdos87`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos87`).
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `ContainsSubgraph` has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] `graphRamseyNumber` has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] `diagRamsey` has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] `graphRamseyNumber` and `diagRamsey` are marked `noncomputable`. (`ContainsSubgraph` is a `Prop`, so it does not need `noncomputable`.)
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `ContainsSubgraph`.
- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase`: `graphRamseyNumber`, `diagRamsey`.
- [x] No local notation used (none needed here).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Both statements use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_87` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
- [x] Both theorems have docstrings referencing the source paper (`[Er95, p. 14]`).

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks "Is it true that …?" — this is a yes/no question, so **Case 1** applies.

- [x] Main theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (Case 1 pattern).
- [x] Variant theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (Case 1 pattern).
- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked, so `answer(True)` would mean "yes".
- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ ε`, `∃ K`, `∀ k`, `∀ {V}`, etc.) appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Category value is `research open` — valid.
- [x] Both `research open` theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (`5`).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number — trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Both open conjectures use `by` / `sorry` (multi-line form with indented `sorry` — acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant named `erdos_87.variants.strong` (correct naming convention).
- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research open, AMS 5]` attributes.
- [x] Variant has a docstring describing the stronger form of the conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `∧`, `↔`, `→`, `ℝ`, `ℕ`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (<100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review (requires build environment).
- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.

---

**Result: PASS** — All checkable items pass. The file is clean, well-structured, and fully conformant. The only unchecked boxes are build verification items that require running `lake build`.

content.text: The review passed (**Result: PASS**). No changes needed.
